scope: 3D_test only. Plant visual families for procedural placeholders and future GLB asset search.
air_quality_caveat: Plants are not treated as the primary air-quality system in calculations. They are used as visual, restorative, and possible shading/comfort-support elements.

placement_rules.small: shelf, sill, hanging pot, or small side table; avoid active door path
placement_rules.medium: floor pot or plant ladder shelf; keep 0.75-0.90 m clear walking strip
placement_rules.large: floor corner or side of glazed wall only; avoid blocking window/door operation
placement_rules.trailing: hanging rail or upper shelf with drip tray; keep away from handles and openings

orientation_rules.wall_adjacent_plants: foliage should face into the room, not into the wall; procedural leaves use a positive room-side bias
orientation_rules.shelf_plants: pots sit on shelf surface, not floor; shelf is wall-hosted with brackets
orientation_rules.hanging_plants: pots hang from a rail with visible suspension lines and should stay beside the opening path
orientation_rules.floor_plants: large plants sit on the floor near corners or beside openings only when circulation is clear

scale_system.mini: 0.35-0.55 m visual height; secondary shelf/sill plants only, not a main retrofit proposal
scale_system.small: 0.55-0.90 m visual height; readable shelf, hanging, or low stand plants
scale_system.medium: 0.90-1.50 m visual height; plant ladder or floor cluster plants
scale_system.large: 1.50-2.30 m visual height; floor corner or side cluster, design-significant vegetation
scale_system.trailing: vines drop 0.45-1.10 m from shelf or hanging rail

visual_styles.rosette_succulent: short radial leaves, small pot, shelf-safe
visual_styles.arching_spider: thin arching leaves, good for hanging/shelf
visual_styles.broadleaf_cluster: medium oval leaves in grouped stems
visual_styles.tall_palm: tall stem with radial fronds, floor-only
visual_styles.small_tree: woody stem with upper canopy, floor-only

project_relevance_note: For HVRA, vegetation should be shown at a scale that can affect perception, shading adjacency, or biophilic comfort. Very small plants are secondary details and should not be presented as main retrofit options.
